meta.description: Primary interface. Receives Telegram messages, authorises the chat, routes commands (/help /status /research /emails /image /cyber) and replies. New commands are added by extending the Command Router switch — easy to add (Telegram Assistant Requirements). Every external call has a failure path (Error Handling).

nodes.name: Telegram Trigger
nodes.notes: Inbound message entry point. Credentials configured in n8n (no token in repo).

nodes.name: Authorise Chat
nodes.notes: Access control: only chat IDs in TELEGRAM_ALLOWED_CHAT_IDS proceed.

nodes.name: Command Router
nodes.notes: Add a new command by adding an output here and wiring a handler. Intelligence commands (/cyber, /opportunities, /energy) are registry-driven; the same Latest Intelligence handler serves any product. Fallback routes to the AI assistant for natural-language intent.

assignments.name: reply
assignments.value:
Jarvis commands:
/help — this message
/status — system status
/research <topic>
/emails — inbox summary
/image <prompt>
/cyber — latest cyber threat brief
/opportunities [org|date] — latest cyber opportunities brief (+ history/search)
/energy [org|date] — latest energy intelligence brief (+ history/search)

nodes.name: AI Assistant (provider-abstracted)
nodes.notes: Default provider is Ollama. Switching AI_PROVIDER points this at the resolved endpoint (AI Provider Abstraction).

nodes.name: Latest Intelligence Brief
nodes.notes: Handles /opportunities and /energy (and /cyber). Latest edition, history, or org/date search. Failure path -> Failure Notice.

nodes.name: Failure Notice

parameters.text: ⚠️ Jarvis hit an error handling that request. The team has been notified and it has been logged.

nodes.notes: Failure path: AI/Ollama unavailable -> tell the user gracefully (Error Handling).
